CONTENT.Career: Ben Mustapha was voted Ligue Professionnelle 1 player of the month for August 2009 by a fansite.He was called up to play for Tunisia for the 2010 Africa Cup of Nations but did not play a match in the tournament.In June 2018 he was named in Tunisia's 23-man squad for the 2018 FIFA World Cup in Russia. He was the substitute goalkeeper in the first Tunisia World Cup game versus England on 18 June 2018.
CONTENT.Honours: CA BizertinTunisian Cup: 2012–13Club AfricainTunisian Ligue Professionnelle 1: 2014–15Tunisian Cup: 2016–17TunisiaAfrican Nations Championship: 2011African Cup of Nations: fourth place 2019IndividualSaudi Professional League Goalkeeper of the Month: January 2020

SUMMARY: Farouk Ben Mustapha (Arabic: فَارُوق بَن مُصطَفى; born 1 July 1989) is a Tunisian former professional footballer who played as a goalkeeper.
